output:
# Research Analysis: PCRC -- Disentangling Proposal Coverage from Global-Coherence Correction in Hybrid Energy-Based Long-Horizon Event-Sequence Prediction

Here is a high-level summarized insight of a research field Machine Learning.

The source is a METHOD paper on long-horizon prediction of event sequences:
HYPRO (Xue, Tan, Mei, et al., NeurIPS 2022) is a HYBRIDLY normalized model that
first uses a LOCALLY normalized autoregressive temporal-point-process base q
(an attentive-neural-Hawkes / Transformer-Hawkes / RMTPP-style sampler) to draw
K candidate future sequences over a long horizon, then uses a GLOBALLY
normalized energy model -- trained by noise-contrastive estimation with negative
samples drawn from that same base q, in the residual-EBM tradition of Deng 2020
-- to rerank the candidates and emit the selected future. We name the
load-bearing weakness PCRC -- the Proposal-Coupled Reranking Confound -- because
that single frozen base q is never decoupled across the THREE roles it plays at
once: the test-time candidate generator (the reranker can only ever select
within supp(q)), the NCE noise distribution (so the energy model only sees
contrasts present in q-samples and is structurally blind to any long-horizon
failure mode that all q-samples share, exactly where NCE consistency
[Gutmann-Hyvarinen 2010] needs the noise to cover the data), and the
global-normalization reference measure (whose partition function is uncomputable
[Lin-McCarthy 2022], making the learned energy q-relative and sample-dependent).
The load-bearing consequence is that HYPRO's reported long-horizon reranking
gain CONFLATES (G) the genuine global-coherence correction the energy model adds
with (C) the base model's proposal coverage, with no diagnostic to tell "the
energy model has nothing left to fix" apart from "the energy model is blind
because the proposal never exposed the failure," and no mechanism to escape the
resulting proposal-coverage ceiling.

The reframe: the base proposal q is not a fixed given but ONE limiting setting
of an explicit, controllable proposal operator pi. We add identity-default
coverage knobs -- a tempered base q^(1/tau), a learned residual mixture
(1-eps)*q + eps*r, and a coverage-corrected NCE that importance-reweights the
widened negatives -- and a 2x2 [frozen-q vs widened-pi] x [no-energy vs energy]
estimator that MEASURES G and C separately for the first time. At eps=0, tau=1,
and unit importance weights, pi reduces to q and the corrected NCE reduces to
standard NCE, so the method provably recomputes HYPRO's exact energy-reranked
prediction within floating-point tolerance with no retraining -- the B1
do-no-harm collapse. Throughout, collapse identities are tagged
[provable-by-construction] and every accuracy gain (e.g., on DILATE-style
shape-and-time long-horizon error, Le Guen-Thome 2019) is tagged
[conjectured -> measured]. This is a benchmarking-methodology contribution: its
metrics are research-context results, not performance claims for clinical
decision support, law enforcement, or operational mobility deployment.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

## 1. Background and Current State of the Art

Temporal point processes (TPPs) are the standard probabilistic language for
sequences of events that arrive at irregular, continuous times -- a sale, a
hospital visit, a social post, a network packet. A TPP is specified by its
conditional intensity function lambda*(t), the instantaneous event rate given the
history, and the whole apparatus of likelihood, compensators, and simulation
follows from it [1]. The foundational self- and mutually-exciting model is the
Hawkes process [2], in which each past event transiently raises the intensity of
future events through an additive triggering kernel; its multivariate form
captures cross-type excitation among many event streams [3]. Because the
likelihood involves an integral of the intensity, exact simulation is itself
nontrivial, and the thinning construction of Lewis and Shedler [4] -- accept/reject
sampling against a dominating Poisson rate -- remains the workhorse for drawing
event sequences from an intensity model. These classical models are
interpretable but rigidly parametric: the triggering kernel is fixed in form, so
they struggle with the long, nonlinear dependencies real event streams exhibit.

The neural turn replaced the hand-designed kernel with a learned encoder of the
event history. Recurrent marked temporal point processes (RMTPP) embed the
history into a vector with a recurrent network -- typically an LSTM [5] -- and read
the next inter-event time and mark off that hidden state [6], and a large family
of attentive and Transformer-Hawkes variants followed, using self-attention over
past events to model far richer excitation patterns. Neural TPPs have been pushed
into high-stakes sequence domains such as electronic health records, where the
event "marks" are diagnoses or interventions and the timing carries clinical
meaning [7]. Crucially for what follows, essentially all of these neural TPP
models are LOCALLY normalized and autoregressive: they factorize the joint
sequence likelihood into a product of per-event conditionals, each a properly
normalized distribution over the next event, and they are trained by maximum
likelihood one step at a time. This local normalization is what makes them
tractable to train and to sample by thinning -- and, as we will see, it is also
the source of their long-horizon weakness.

Long-horizon forecasting of event sequences -- predicting not the next event but
an entire future stretch of many events -- exposes that weakness sharply [8].
Rolling out a locally-normalized autoregressive TPP many steps ahead feeds the
model's own sampled events back as conditioning history, so any per-step error
compounds: the model is trained on ground-truth history (teacher forcing) but
deployed on self-generated history, the classic exposure-bias mismatch. The
general limitations of autoregressive models have been characterized formally --
there are distributions an autoregressive factorization cannot represent or can
only represent with pathological conditionals [9], and a broader analysis of
where autoregressive sequence models break down and what alternatives buy you has
been laid out at length [10]. The upshot for TPPs is that a purely local,
step-by-step generator has no representation of GLOBAL sequence coherence -- it
never scores a candidate future as a whole -- which motivates adding a globally
aware corrector on top of the autoregressive base.

Energy-based models (EBMs) are the natural tool for that global corrector. An EBM
assigns an unnormalized scalar energy to a whole configuration and defines a
Gibbs distribution proportional to exp(-energy); the idea traces back to
associative-memory networks whose dynamics descend an energy landscape [11], and
the modern view of energy-based learning -- scoring whole structured outputs
rather than normalizing locally -- is consolidated in the energy-based learning
tutorial [12]. The central obstacle is the partition function: training an EBM by
maximum likelihood needs the intractable normalizer, so the field developed ways
around it. Contrastive divergence approximates the likelihood gradient with short
Markov-chain sampling [13]; implicit-generation EBMs train high-dimensional
energies with Langevin sampling and a replay buffer [14]. The route most relevant
here is noise-contrastive estimation (NCE) [15], which sidesteps the normalizer
entirely by training the model to discriminate true data from samples of a known
NOISE distribution. NCE carries a load-bearing theoretical condition that is the
crux of this proposal: its consistency guarantee requires the NOISE distribution
to COVER the support of the data -- if the noise places (near) zero probability on
a region where data occur, the classifier never has to score that region and the
learned model is left unconstrained there.

The direct architectural template for HYPRO is the residual energy-based model for
text generation of Deng et al. [16]: a globally-normalized energy is placed on TOP
of a locally-normalized autoregressive base language model, and it is trained by
NCE using the base model's own samples as the negatives. The base supplies fluent
candidates; the residual energy re-scores them for global properties the local
model misses. This "locally-normalized base + globally-normalized NCE energy"
recipe is exactly the hybrid that HYPRO transplants into the long-horizon TPP
setting. It also inherits a hard theoretical limit: exact global normalization
over sequences is impossible in general -- the partition function of an
energy-based sequence model is uncomputable [17] -- which is precisely why these
models never normalize globally and instead fall back to sampling and reranking a
finite candidate set.

HYPRO [18] is the synthesis. It is a HYBRIDLY normalized probabilistic model for
long-horizon prediction of event sequences: a locally-normalized autoregressive
TPP base q (an attentive-neural-Hawkes / Transformer-Hawkes / RMTPP-style sampler)
first draws K candidate future sequences over the horizon by thinning [4], and a
globally-normalized energy model -- trained by NCE [15] with negative samples drawn
from that same base q, in the residual-EBM tradition of Deng et al. [16] -- then
reranks the K candidates and emits the selected future. Training the energy uses
standard stochastic optimization such as Adam [20]. Because exact long-horizon
accuracy is not captured by next-event likelihood alone, long-horizon TPP work is
read through distortion measures that separate WHEN events happen from the SHAPE
of the event sequence; the DILATE shape-and-time distortion loss [19] is the
canonical lens for disentangling timing error from structural error in long-range
forecasts, and event-sequence benchmarks are drawn from large real network and
interaction corpora such as the Stanford SNAP collection [21] alongside synthetic
Hawkes generators.

The structural blind spot the field has not examined is this. In the residual-EBM
template [16], in HYPRO [18], and in the hybrid locally+globally-normalized recipe
generally, the proposal, the NCE noise, and the global-normalization reference are
ALL the SAME single frozen base distribution q, and that coupling is never
decoupled or measured. The frozen base q in fact plays THREE coupled roles at
once: (i) ROLE i -- TEST-TIME CANDIDATE GENERATOR: the reranker can only ever
select from supp(q); it cannot recover a long-horizon future that q never proposes
among its K samples. (ii) ROLE ii -- NCE NOISE (NEGATIVE) DISTRIBUTION: the
globally-normalized energy is trained with negatives drawn from that same frozen
q, so it only learns contrasts present in q-samples and is STRUCTURALLY BLIND to
any long-horizon failure mode that ALL q-samples share -- exactly the regime where
NCE consistency [15] is violated because the noise fails to cover the data. (iii)
ROLE iii -- GLOBAL-NORMALIZATION REFERENCE MEASURE: with the partition function
uncomputable [17], the energy and its reranking gain are q-relative and
sample-size-dependent. The load-bearing consequence is that HYPRO's reported
long-horizon reranking gain CONFLATES (G) genuine GLOBAL-COHERENCE CORRECTION the
energy model adds with (C) the base model's proposal COVERAGE, with no diagnostic
to separate them and no mechanism to escape the resulting proposal-coverage
ceiling. We name this the Proposal-Coupled Reranking Confound (PCRC).

## 2. Key Findings and Analysis

2.1  The analytical core: one frozen q, three coupled roles.
The load-bearing object is the single FROZEN locally-normalized autoregressive
temporal-point-process base q. In HYPRO it plays THREE coupled roles that the
design never decouples: ROLE i -- TEST-TIME CANDIDATE GENERATOR (the reranker can
only ever select within supp(q)); ROLE ii -- NCE NOISE (NEGATIVE) DISTRIBUTION
(the energy is trained by noise-contrastive estimation with negatives drawn from
that SAME frozen q, so it is structurally blind to any long-horizon failure mode
that all q-samples share); and ROLE iii -- GLOBAL-NORMALIZATION REFERENCE MEASURE
(the partition function over sequences is uncomputable, so the energy and its gain
are q-relative and sample-size-dependent). Because the same q drives generation,
contrast, and normalization at once, HYPRO's reported long-horizon "reranking
gain" CONFLATES (G) the genuine global-coherence correction the energy model adds
with (C) the base model's proposal coverage, and the design provides NO diagnostic
to separate them. This is the Proposal-Coupled Reranking Confound (PCRC).

2.2  Why naive fix (a) "just increase K" provably cannot help with coverage.
Coverage is a SUPPORT property of q, not a sample-count property. If a long-horizon
mode (e.g., a burst / regime-switch future) carries near-zero mass under q, then
drawing more candidates K only redraws the high-mass modes q already favors -- the
extra samples concentrate where q already is, and the probability of ever sampling
the near-zero-mass mode stays vanishingly small. The reranker's selection set is a
subset of supp(q) for any finite K (ROLE i), so no amount of K lets it select a
future q never proposes. Increasing K spends compute to sharpen estimates of modes
that are already covered; it does nothing for the modes that define the coverage
ceiling. Hence "sample more" cannot convert a coverage limit into a coherence gain.

2.3  Why naive fix (b) "just train the energy longer / bigger" provably cannot help.
NCE consistency (Gutmann-Hyvarinen 2010) REQUIRES the noise distribution to cover
the data support: the contrastive objective only produces a learning signal where
the noise places mass. In the regions where every q-sample is absent (exactly the
long-horizon modes the base misses, ROLE ii), the noise mass is ~zero, so the NCE
gradient is ~zero there for ALL training time and ALL network sizes. A structural
blind spot in the contrast set cannot be trained away by more epochs or more
parameters -- there is simply no gradient to descend. Bigger/longer energy training
refines the decision boundary among modes q exposes; it cannot manufacture a
boundary against a mode that never appears as a negative.

2.4  Why naive fix (c) "just improve the base" is self-defeating and bounded.
If the base were perfect, no reranker would be needed -- improving q toward
perfection dissolves the very setting HYPRO targets. More concretely, the base is
autoregressive and therefore inherits inherent long-horizon EXPOSURE-BIAS limits
(Lin 2021): teacher-forced training never conditions on its own multi-step rollouts,
so error compounds over a long horizon and certain long-range modes are
systematically under-covered. "Just improve the base" is thus both a moving target
(any residual miss reopens the same PCRC) and bounded by a known structural limit
of autoregressive sampling. The point of the reframe is not to make q perfect but
to make its coverage a CONTROLLABLE, MEASURABLE quantity.

2.5  Why the gain number itself is not coverage-invariant (fix-resistance of ROLE iii).
The global energy's partition function over sequences is uncomputable
(Lin-McCarthy 2022), forcing a sample-based approximation whose reference measure
IS q (ROLE iii). Consequently the learned energy, and the scalar "reranking gain"
read off from it, are q-relative and sample-size-dependent: change the proposal or
the sample budget and the same underlying coherence correction reports a different
number. This is why one cannot simply compare raw gain scalars across proposals to
decide whether G or C moved -- the measurement instrument (the gain itself) shifts
with the proposal. PCRC is therefore not a tuning nuisance but a measurement
identification problem: G and C are not separately observable through the gain
scalar as HYPRO defines it.

2.6  The reframe mechanics: q as one setting of a controllable proposal operator pi.
We treat q not as a fixed given but as ONE limiting setting of an explicit,
controllable PROPOSAL OPERATOR pi, equipped with identity-default coverage knobs:
  - tempered base q^(1/tau): raising tau > 1 flattens the sampling distribution
    toward dispersed/under-covered regions; tau=1 recovers q exactly.
  - learned residual mixture pi = (1-eps)*q + eps*r: a small mixing weight eps
    routes a fraction of candidates through a residual proposal r designed to reach
    modes the base misses; eps=0 recovers q exactly. Concretely r is a
    dispersion-augmented / burst-aware sampler (e.g., a higher-temperature or
    over-dispersed variant of the base, or a learned mode-seeking proposal that
    targets high-residual-energy / high-disagreement regions) -- chosen to be
    mode-AGNOSTIC (it widens coverage without being told which specific mode to hit).
  - COVERAGE-CORRECTED NCE: when negatives are widened from q to pi, the NCE
    objective importance-reweights each negative by a self-normalized weight
    w = pi/q (or, equivalently, uses the ranking-NCE variant) so the energy estimate
    stays calibrated despite negatives being drawn from pi != q. With
    importance-weights = 1 it reduces to standard NCE exactly. Self-normalization
    (weights normalized within the negative batch) keeps the estimator well-behaved
    and removes the unknown proposal-normalizer.

2.7  The 2x2 estimator as the disentangling instrument.
We run a 2x2 [frozen-q vs widened-pi] x [no-energy vs energy] estimator whose four
cells isolate the two effects:
  - frozen-q,   no-energy = base autoregressive prediction (the COVERAGE FLOOR).
  - frozen-q,   energy    = HYPRO (energy reranking on frozen coverage).
  - widened-pi, no-energy = the coverage effect C IN ISOLATION (energy off).
  - widened-pi, energy    = full method (G measured ON TOP of widened coverage).
ROW contrast (energy on vs off) isolates G -- the global-coherence correction the
energy adds at fixed coverage. COLUMN contrast (q vs pi) isolates C -- what widening
coverage buys with the energy held fixed. For the first time G and C are read off
two orthogonal contrasts instead of one entangled gain scalar.

2.8  The collapse identity as the B1 unit test.
COLLAPSE IDENTITY [provable-by-construction]: At eps=0, tau=1, importance-weights=1,
pi reduces to the frozen base q and the corrected NCE reduces to standard NCE,
recomputing HYPRO's energy-reranked prediction within floating-point tolerance
(max abs diff <= 1e-6) with no retraining -- the B1 do-no-harm unit test. This makes
the entire reframe a strict generalization of HYPRO: at the off setting the
[widened-pi, energy] cell IS HYPRO, guaranteeing no regression, and every knob
turned away from default is an audited, reversible widening rather than a new model.

## 3. Novel Insights and Recommendations

The central novel insight is that HYPRO's apparent simplicity hides a triple
identification problem: one frozen autoregressive base q is silently doing three
jobs at once -- it is the test-time candidate generator (ROLE i), the NCE noise /
negative distribution (ROLE ii), and the global-normalization reference measure
(ROLE iii) -- and because the design never decouples them, the single number it
reports as a long-horizon "reranking gain" is not what it appears to be. That gain
CONFLATES (G) the genuine global-coherence correction the energy model adds with
(C) the base model's proposal coverage. The two are not merely hard to separate;
they are not separately observable through the gain scalar as HYPRO defines it,
because ROLE iii makes the gain q-relative and sample-dependent (Section 2.5). The
consequence is a proposal-coverage ceiling that masquerades as success: a hybrid
model can report a clean gain while remaining structurally blind to exactly the
rare long-horizon modes that all q-samples share, with no way to tell "the energy
model has nothing left to fix" apart from "the energy model is blind because the
proposal never exposed the failure."

The second insight is methodological and reusable beyond TPPs. Reframing q as one
limiting setting of an explicit, controllable proposal operator pi -- with the
identity-default knobs tau and eps and a coverage-corrected NCE -- converts the
abstract NCE-consistency premise "assume the noise covers the data" [15] from an
untestable footnote into a knob you can turn and a coverage gap you can measure.
The 2x2 [frozen-q vs widened-pi] x [no-energy vs energy] estimator is then a new,
general measurement instrument: holding one factor fixed while moving the other
reads G off the row contrast (energy on/off) and C off the column contrast (q vs
pi). Nothing in that instrument is specific to event sequences. Any
generate-then-rerank pipeline -- a residual-EBM text generator [16] reranking
samples from a frozen language model, a reward model performing best-of-K
selection over a frozen policy, any learned scorer placed atop a fixed proposal --
inherits the identical G-vs-C entanglement, and the same 2x2 decomposition plus
coverage-ceiling diagnostic transplants directly. PCRC is, in effect, a property
of the phrase "rerank samples from a frozen base," not of TPPs.

The third insight is what makes the reframe safe to adopt: it is a strict
generalization of HYPRO, not a competing model. The collapse identity is exact and
[provable-by-construction]: at eps=0, tau=1, and unit importance weights, pi
reduces to the frozen base q and the coverage-corrected NCE reduces to standard
NCE, so the [widened-pi, energy] cell recomputes HYPRO's energy-reranked prediction
within floating-point tolerance (max abs diff <= 1e-6) with no retraining -- the B1
do-no-harm unit test. Every knob turned away from its default is therefore an
audited, reversible widening rather than a new architecture, and the off-setting is
a literal equality to the source method that any reviewer can run as a unit test
before trusting a single accuracy number. This do-no-harm guarantee is what lets
the diagnostic be deployed without risk of regression: in the worst case the method
is HYPRO exactly.

We therefore recommend a concrete change to how the community reports reranking and
reward-model gains. First, report G and C SEPARATELY using the 2x2 contrasts, and
exhibit the exact collapse-to-source as a new evidentiary standard: a hybrid model
should demonstrate (a) that it recomputes its base reranker at the off setting
within floating-point tolerance, and (b) how much of its headline gain survives
when coverage is held fixed (the row contrast) versus when the energy is held fixed
(the column contrast). A single number measured against an unverified base should no
longer be accepted as evidence that "our reranker helps" [conjectured -> measured].
Second, to keep a coverage ceiling from hiding as well-calibrated-looking
incompleteness, report per-mark and per-event-type coverage, worst-group
long-horizon error stratified by event-frequency quintile, and per-horizon
calibration curves with uncertainty / credible intervals, so that rare-mode failure
is explicitly visible rather than averaged away. Third, on the data and scope side,
use only de-identified, standard-licensed public benchmarks with aggregate-level
reporting, and scope all claims to research/benchmark settings: the coverage-corrected
proposal operator pi must not be read as a license for clinical decision support,
predictive policing, or operational mobility deployment, each of which carries
independent obligations for consent, demographic auditing, and regulatory compliance
beyond the scope of this work.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[Question 1] - What is the problem?**

In long-horizon prediction of event sequences, where HYPRO reranks K candidate
futures sampled from a single frozen locally-normalized autoregressive
temporal-point-process base q using a globally-normalized energy model trained
by noise-contrastive estimation whose negative samples are also drawn from q --
so that q simultaneously plays three coupled and never-decoupled roles, the
test-time candidate generator (the reranker can only ever select within
supp(q)), the NCE noise distribution (the energy is structurally blind to any
long-horizon failure mode that all q-samples share), and the
uncomputable-partition-function global-normalization reference measure -- can we
resolve the resulting Proposal-Coupled Reranking Confound (PCRC), in which
HYPRO's reported reranking gain conflates genuine global-coherence correction
added by the energy model (G) with the base model's proposal coverage (C) while
providing no diagnostic to separate them, by reframing q as one limiting setting
of an explicit controllable proposal operator pi equipped with identity-default
coverage knobs (a tempered base q^(1/tau), a learned residual mixture
(1-eps)*q + eps*r, and a coverage-corrected NCE) and a 2x2 [frozen-q vs
widened-pi] x [no-energy vs energy] estimator that measures G and C separately,
while by construction recomputing HYPRO's exact energy-reranked prediction within
floating-point tolerance and with no retraining at eps=0, tau=1, and unit
importance weights?

**[Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?**

Resolving PCRC matters first because long-horizon event forecasting is deployed
in high-stakes domains where a hidden coverage ceiling produces confident-but-
systematically-incomplete forecasts. In healthcare event streams [7], in demand
and financial event flows, and in social and mobility traces [21], the decisions
that hurt are the rare long-horizon modes -- a clinical deterioration cascade, a
demand spike, a regime switch. If the autoregressive base q never proposes such a
mode among its K candidates (ROLE i) and all q-samples share that omission, then
the energy reranker is STRUCTURALLY BLIND to it (ROLE ii) and the system reports a
clean reranking gain while never being able to surface the event that matters.
The danger is not loud error; it is quiet, well-calibrated-looking incompleteness.
A diagnostic that exposes the proposal-coverage ceiling C separately from the
genuine global-coherence correction G tells a practitioner whether their model has
"nothing left to fix" or is "blind because the proposal never exposed the
failure" -- two situations that current hybrid models cannot distinguish.

Second, the conflation is not specific to TPPs; it is a general property of the
ENTIRE class of residual-EBM and hybrid locally+globally-normalized sequence
models, and of any generate-then-rerank or reward-model pipeline. The residual-
EBM text generator [16] reranks samples from a frozen base language model; reward-
model and best-of-K reranking pipelines rerank samples from a frozen policy. In
every such design the reported "reranker gain" or "reward-model gain" mixes G
(what the scorer genuinely corrects) with C (what the frozen generator happened to
cover). Because the G-vs-C entanglement is a property of "rerank samples from a
frozen base" in general, a diagnostic that separates them -- the 2x2 [frozen-q vs
widened-pi] x [no-energy vs energy] estimator -- is broadly reusable far beyond
event sequences, wherever a fixed proposal feeds a learned scorer.

Third, the work turns an abstract theoretical condition into a measurable
instrument. NCE consistency [15] is usually stated as a footnote -- "assume the
noise covers the data." PCRC shows that in the hybrid design this assumption is
not free: it is violated EXACTLY on the long-horizon failure modes shared by all
q-samples, and ROLE iii's uncomputable partition function [17] means the resulting
distortion cannot be normalized away. Reframing q as one limiting setting of a
controllable proposal operator pi -- with identity-default knobs (tempered base
q^(1/tau), residual mixture (1-eps)*q + eps*r, coverage-corrected NCE) -- converts
"does the noise cover the data?" from an untestable premise into a knob you can
turn and a coverage gap you can measure against synthetic ORACLE modes.

Finally, a clean G/C decomposition with a collapse-to-source baseline changes how
the community should REPORT reranking and reward-model gains. Today a hybrid model
reports a single number against an unverified base. With the 2x2 estimator, the
[frozen-q, energy] cell IS HYPRO and the [frozen-q, no-energy] cell is the
coverage floor, so any claimed gain is measured over a provable equality rather
than an opaque baseline; the column contrast isolates C and the row contrast
isolates G [conjectured->measured]. The future-research impact is explicit:
reranking papers across modalities can be asked to report G and C separately and
to exhibit their exact collapse to the source method, raising the evidentiary bar
for "our reranker helps."

**[Question 3] - Why is it hard?**

The difficulty is that separating G (global-coherence correction) from C (proposal
coverage) runs into five concrete obstacles, each of which defeats a straightforward
approach.

O1. Coverage of long-horizon modes is UNOBSERVABLE on real event data.
There is no ground-truth set of "modes the base missed" on Amazon, Taobao, NYC Taxi,
StackOverflow, Retweet, or MIMIC-II sequences -- we never see the data-generating
process, only realized sequences. So on real data we cannot directly verify that a
gain came from covering a previously-missed mode (C) rather than from coherence (G).
RESOLUTION DIRECTION: build SYNTHETIC oracles (Hawkes, self-correcting, inhomogeneous
Poisson) where the generative modes are KNOWN and a missed mode can be PLANTED, so the
G/C split can be validated against ground truth, and only then estimate the same
quantities on real data via density-ratio / two-sample diagnostics.

O2. Widening the proposal trades coverage for VARIANCE (a bias-variance frontier).
The coverage-corrected NCE reweights widened negatives by w = pi/q. When pi reaches
into near-zero-mass regions of q, these importance weights can BLOW UP, and
self-normalization introduces a finite-sample BIAS. So naive widening that "fixes"
coverage can wreck the energy estimate's variance -- the cure can be noisier than the
disease. There is a genuine bias-variance frontier: too little widening leaves the
blind spot (ROLE ii); too much widening destabilizes the estimator (ROLE iii). The
method must traverse this frontier (weight clipping / self-normalization / modest eps,
tau) rather than maximize coverage blindly.

O3. Constructing r is a chicken-and-egg problem.
The residual proposal r must reach modes the base MISSES -- but if we already knew
which long-horizon mode was missed, we would simply fix the base. So r cannot be told
the target; it must be MODE-AGNOSTIC (dispersion-/burst-augmented or
disagreement-seeking) yet still effective at exposing the right failures. Designing a
proposal that systematically surfaces unknown misses without degenerating into pure
noise is the central engineering risk.

O4. The reranking-gain scalar is q-relative and sample-dependent (not comparable).
Because the partition function is uncomputable (Lin-McCarthy 2022) and is approximated
relative to the proposal (ROLE iii), the raw gain scalar shifts when the proposal or
sample budget changes. So we cannot just compare "gain under q" to "gain under pi" --
the instrument moved. RESOLUTION DIRECTION: define a COVERAGE-INVARIANT gain (gain
measured at MATCHED effective sample size and reported via the orthogonal 2x2
contrasts, not a single proposal-relative number) so that G and C are comparable
across settings.

O5. Long-horizon error is multi-dimensional; a single OTD scalar conflates sources.
A long-horizon future has at least three error axes -- event COUNT, inter-event TIMING,
and MARKS/types. A single optimal-transport-distance (OTD) scalar mixes all three, so
a gain could come from better counts, better timing, or better marks, and we could not
tell which the energy fixed. RESOLUTION DIRECTION: a DECOMPOSED metric (OTD split into
count/time/mark components, plus DILATE shape-vs-time distortion) so the G/C split is
itself reported per error axis.

Why straightforward approaches fail to separate G from C. "Bigger K" cannot move C
because coverage is a support property (Section 2.2); "bigger/longer energy net"
cannot move the blind spot because NCE has no gradient where the noise has ~zero mass
(Section 2.3); "ad hoc temperature" widens coverage but, without the coverage-corrected
NCE and the 2x2 contrasts, it changes BOTH the candidate set AND the q-relative gain
instrument at once -- so any resulting change remains an entangled G+C number, not a
separation. Only an estimator that holds one factor fixed while moving the other (the
2x2), with a calibration correction (w = pi/q) and an oracle to validate against, can
identify G and C.

**[Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?**

The barrier is that every prior route in this lineage keeps the proposal welded to
the frozen base and never treats it as a measurable, controllable object. HYPRO
[18] and the residual-EBM template it descends from [16] improve the system along
two axes only: they increase K (draw more candidate futures) and they enlarge the
energy model's capacity. Both axes leave the proposal IDENTICAL to the frozen base
q. More samples from q cannot cover a long-horizon mode q never proposes (ROLE i
is untouched), and a bigger energy trained on q-negatives stays blind to any
failure mode all q-samples share (ROLE ii is untouched). Neither line ever
measures coverage, so the proposal-coverage ceiling C is invisible by
construction and the reported gain silently conflates G with C.

There is a near-miss family that DOES change the noise or proposal -- and it is
important to position against it honestly. Adaptive-noise and learned-proposal NCE
lines (flow-contrastive and conditional/self-adversarial NCE, ranking-style NCE)
adapt the noise to improve the estimator; cooperative-learning and implicit-EBM
proposal refinement [14] and persistent/contrastive-divergence sampling [13]
refine the negative sampler to make EBM TRAINING more efficient or the samples
more faithful. These are genuine prior art on "do not use a fixed naive noise."
But -- stated clearly -- NONE of them reframes the proposal as a COVERAGE operator
with a G-vs-C disentangling estimator, NONE provides a measurable proposal-
coverage CEILING, and NONE preserves an EXACT collapse to the source reranker in
the long-horizon TPP setting. They optimize sample fidelity or training speed;
they do not ask "how much of the reranking gain is coverage C versus correction
G," and they do not instrument ROLE i, ROLE ii, and ROLE iii as three coupled
roles of one object.

Two compounding obstacles explain the gap. First, coverage of long-horizon modes
is UNOBSERVABLE on real event data: there is no ground-truth mode set on an EHR or
mobility stream [7,21], so you cannot directly measure whether q's K samples
missed a mode, and without that measurement there is no signal that ROLE ii's NCE-
coverage condition [15] is being violated. Second, widening the proposal naively
backfires: drawing negatives from a distribution other than q breaks the q-
relative energy (ROLE iii), and importance-reweighting to fix it inflates weight
variance, so a careless widening trades a coverage problem for an estimator-
variance problem and can make things worse. The field therefore lacked BOTH a
measurement instrument and a do-no-harm reframe at the same time.

Our approach differs and improves on exactly these points, and we make it clear.
(1) We introduce an explicit proposal operator pi with identity-default coverage
knobs -- tempered base q^(1/tau), residual mixture (1-eps)*q + eps*r -- so the
proposal becomes a controllable object rather than a frozen given. (2) We add a
COVERAGE-CORRECTED NCE that importance-reweights the widened negatives so the
energy stays well-defined when the noise moves off q, directly addressing the
ROLE iii / variance obstacle. (3) We build the 2x2 [frozen-q vs widened-pi] x
[no-energy vs energy] estimator that MEASURES G (row contrast, energy on/off) and
C (column contrast, q vs pi) SEPARATELY for the first time. (4) We make coverage
OBSERVABLE with synthetic ORACLE modes -- a controlled generator whose long-horizon
modes are known by construction -- so the proposal-coverage ceiling can be read off
directly. (5) We guarantee do-no-harm with an exact B1 collapse: At eps=0, tau=1,
importance-weights=1, pi reduces to the frozen base q and the corrected NCE reduces
to standard NCE, recomputing HYPRO's energy-reranked prediction within floating-
point tolerance (max abs diff <= 1e-6) with no retraining -- the B1 do-no-harm unit
test [provable-by-construction]. No prior adaptive-noise or learned-proposal method
offers this combination of a coverage operator, a coverage-corrected estimator, a
G-vs-C decomposition, oracle-measured coverage, and an exact source collapse in the
long-horizon TPP setting.

**[Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?**

METHOD.
- Proposal operator pi with knobs tau, eps. pi generalizes the frozen base q:
  tempered base q^(1/tau) (tau=1 -> q); learned residual mixture
  pi = (1-eps)*q + eps*r (eps=0 -> q). The residual proposal r is a mode-agnostic
  widener: a dispersion-augmented / burst-aware sampler (higher-temperature or
  over-dispersed base variant) and, as a learned option, a disagreement/mode-seeking
  proposal that allocates candidates to high-residual-energy regions -- it widens
  supp toward under-covered long-horizon modes WITHOUT being told the target mode.
- Coverage-corrected NCE objective. When negatives are drawn from pi != q, each
  negative is importance-reweighted by a self-normalized weight w = pi/q (the
  ranking-NCE variant is the drop-in alternative). EXACT REDUCTION: at
  importance-weights = 1 the objective is standard NCE term-for-term. Self-normalized
  weights (normalized within the negative batch) and weight clipping control variance
  on the bias-variance frontier (O2).
- 2x2 estimator. [frozen-q vs widened-pi] x [no-energy vs energy], four cells:
  base coverage floor / HYPRO / coverage effect C alone / full method. Row contrast =
  G; column contrast = C (Section 2.7).
- NEW coverage-ceiling diagnostic. Define the coverage ceiling as the FRACTION of the
  held-out data's long-horizon structure (its modes / event-count and burst statistics)
  that lies in NEAR-ZERO-MASS regions of the proposal. On SYNTHETIC processes with
  known generative modes this is measurable EXACTLY (compare planted-mode mass under
  pi vs q). On REAL data it is estimable via two-sample / density-ratio tests between
  generated candidates and held-out sequences (the fraction of held-out long-horizon
  statistics the proposal assigns ~zero density to). The diagnostic turns "is there a
  coverage ceiling?" from an intuition into a measured number.
- Coverage-invariant gain. Report gain at MATCHED effective sample size and as the
  orthogonal 2x2 contrasts rather than a single proposal-relative scalar (O4), so G and
  C are comparable across proposals.

DATASETS.
- SYNTHETIC with ORACLE missed-mode sets (the identification backbone): Hawkes
  processes (self- and mutually-exciting; Hawkes 1971 / Liniger / Daley-Vere-Jones),
  self-correcting processes, and inhomogeneous Poisson via thinning (Lewis-Shedler
  1979). In each we PLANT a long-horizon mode (e.g., a burst / regime-switch) that the
  autoregressive base under-covers, so the planted G/C split is KNOWN and the 2x2
  estimate can be validated against ground truth.
- REAL marked event-sequence benchmarks from the neural-TPP / HYPRO literature:
  Amazon, Taobao, NYC Taxi, StackOverflow, Retweet (SNAP; Leskovec-Krevl 2014), and
  MIMIC-II / EHR (Enguehard 2020) for the marked clinical setting. MIMIC-II
  experiments use the publicly available, de-identified MIMIC-II benchmark dataset;
  no raw clinical notes, no patient identifiers, no re-identification attempts, and
  all results are reported at the aggregate benchmark level. NYC Taxi and SNAP
  retweet datasets use publicly available, spatially/temporally aggregated, or
  anonymized benchmark versions; no real-time individual trajectories or
  user-identifiable social-graph links are used, and all downstream analyses report
  aggregate dataset-level statistics.

METRICS.
- Long-horizon OTD (optimal transport distance) DECOMPOSED into count / time / mark
  components (so the G/C split is reported per error axis; O5).
- DILATE shape-and-time distortion (Le Guen-Thome 2019) for long-horizon shape vs
  timing error.
- Per-horizon CALIBRATION / coverage of predictive intervals. Report per-horizon
  calibration curves (predicted event probability vs empirical frequency for each
  prediction horizon tau) and confidence intervals (95% quantile-based or Bayesian
  credible intervals); a coverage ceiling could produce overly confident incomplete
  predictions, so per-horizon uncertainty must be explicit to downstream users.
- The coverage-ceiling diagnostic (above).
- FAIRNESS / per-subgroup coverage. In addition to the aggregate long-horizon and
  DILATE-style metrics, report per-mark coverage and error analysis (per-event-type
  coverage fraction, per-data-origin-domain error rates) to ensure the
  coverage-corrected proposal operator pi does not simply amplify rare-event modes
  that may encode sensitive demographic or domain imbalances.
- WORST-GROUP error. Report worst-group long-horizon error (maximum error across
  event-mark subgroups) alongside aggregate metrics, with a stratified breakdown by
  event-frequency quintile; long-horizon forecasts on rare events are high-risk and
  must be explicitly visible, not hidden in aggregate averaging.
- HEADLINE: the G/C decomposition of reranking gain from the 2x2.

BASELINES.
- Base autoregressive TPP: RMTPP (Du 2016) and an attentive / Transformer-Hawkes base.
- HYPRO (Xue 2022) -- the COLLAPSE TARGET (B1).
- HYPRO + larger K -- the "sample more" null (tests Section 2.2).
- HYPRO + longer / bigger energy training -- the "train more" null (tests Section 2.3).
- Coverage-only ablation: widen pi with energy OFF (the [widened-pi, no-energy] cell).
- Capacity / compute-matched controls: every widened-pi or bigger-energy condition is
  matched on parameters and on total sample/compute budget against the base, so a win
  cannot be attributed to added compute.

FALSIFIABILITY TRIAD / ABLATIONS (each tied to the claim it can refute).
- B1 EXACT COLLAPSE UNIT TEST [provable-by-construction]. At eps=0, tau=1,
  importance-weights=1, pi reduces to the frozen base q and the corrected NCE reduces
  to standard NCE, recomputing HYPRO's energy-reranked prediction within floating-point
  tolerance (max abs diff <= 1e-6) with no retraining -- the B1 do-no-harm unit test.
  REFUTES: "the reframe changes / breaks HYPRO at the off setting."
- DUAL PLACEBO.
  * Leg-1 (coverage-DESTROYING): widen pi with ENTROPY-MATCHED RANDOM dispersion that
    does NOT target real missed modes (same added entropy as the real widener, but
    pointed nowhere structural). If the measured coverage gain C was genuine missed-mode
    structure, it should VANISH here; if C persists, the "gain" was mere diversity, not
    coverage of real structure. REFUTES: "C reflects real missed-mode coverage."
  * Leg-2 (energy-ABSENT): widened-pi with the energy OFF (the [widened-pi, no-energy]
    cell). Isolates whether coverage ALONE explains the observed gain. REFUTES: "the gain
    needs the energy model (G)" vs "coverage alone (C) explains it."
- DOSE-RESPONSE. Sweep eps up and tau up. C should RISE then PLATEAU as widening
  saturates the data's missed modes (coverage is finite). An UNBOUNDED rise (gain keeps
  climbing with more dispersion) or a FLAT response (no rise at all) FALSIFIES the
  mechanism -- the first says we are just adding diversity, the second says there was no
  coverage deficit to fill. REFUTES: "C is bounded, real, and coverage-driven."

EXPECTED OUTCOMES [conjectured->measured].
- On SYNTHETIC-with-oracle, the 2x2 estimator RECOVERS the planted G/C split (the
  measured row/column contrasts match the known coverage vs coherence shares).
  [conjectured->measured]
- HYPRO's reported reranking gain is shown to be PARTLY C (coverage), not all G -- i.e.,
  some of the published long-horizon gain is the base proposal's coverage, surfaced for
  the first time by the column contrast. [conjectured->measured]
- Coverage-corrected widening LIFTS long-horizon DILATE / OTD on exactly the modes the
  base missed, while the B1 collapse [provable-by-construction] GUARANTEES no regression
  at the off setting (the widened method is a strict generalization). [conjectured->measured]
- The dual placebo and dose-response behave as predicted (Leg-1 C->~0, Leg-2 isolates
  coverage, dose-response rises-then-plateaus), supporting that the recovered C is real
  coverage rather than diversity or compute. [conjectured->measured]
